feature_summary.name: Quick Search
feature_summary.goal: Authenticated users can run quick flight searches from the dedicated quick-search page.

capabilities: Type origin and destination IATA or partial airport queries
Use autocomplete suggestions with mouse or keyboard
See validation feedback when required route data is missing

functional_requirements: All quick-search tests must authenticate first and wait for the private-route auth guard to finish before asserting on the form.
Typing a partial query in an airport field shows autocomplete suggestions.
Selecting a suggestion with the mouse fills the field with the airport IATA code and closes the suggestions list.
Keyboard navigation with ArrowDown and Enter can select an autocomplete suggestion.
Clearing an airport field hides its suggestions list.
Submitting without a route shows a visible validation error on the quick-search page.
Providing a valid route and outbound date enables search submission without inline validation errors.
test_generation_guidance: Use the current quick-search selectors instead of inferred XPath: input[name="origin_iata"], input[name="destination_iata"], #origin-suggestions, #destination-suggestions, button[type="submit"], and .qs-error when asserting inline field errors.
For private routes, wait until the auth guard has completed and the quick-search inputs are visible before interacting with the page.
Mouse-selection tests must type into the field, wait for a visible suggestions list, and click a visible suggestion before asserting the selected value.
Empty-route validation tests must actually submit from the quick-search form, for example by pressing Enter from the origin field or using the submit button, before asserting error text.
Keyboard-selection tests must press ArrowDown and then Enter on input[name="origin_iata"], and then assert the input value changed to a 3-letter IATA code after the key presses.
Valid-search tests must assert the concrete absence of visible .qs-error elements for origin, destination, and outbound date after choosing both route values and a future outbound date.

For empty-route validation, clear both route inputs first, keep the test on /quick-search, submit from the origin field, and assert against the current message copy that starts with 'Please enter a search'.
Prefer direct navigation to /login and /quick-search; avoid homepage-first flows unless the page under test explicitly requires them.
out_of_scope: Dashboard-only shortcuts outside the quick-search flow
